summary:
EARLY MINISTRY AND IMPARTATIONS: Brandon shares his foundational experiences seeking God's presence by traveling extensively to Benny Hinn crusades in places like Fort Lauderdale, Arkansas, and Fort Worth. During one event, after being knocked down by the power of the Holy Spirit, Brandon met Ralph Wilkerson of Melodyland Christian Center. Wilkerson prophesied over Brandon, declaring he was part of an end-time group destined to bring healing to nations, and imparted a double portion of his anointing, comparing Brandon's mantle to those of Kathryn Kuhlman, Oral Roberts, and Benny Hinn. Later, while working as a janitor at a mega-church, Brandon experienced another profound impartation when Reinhard Bonnke laid hands on him, further igniting his spiritual calling and prophetic destiny.

JANITORIAL WORK AND PROPHETIC AWAKENING: Brandon recounts his humble beginnings working as a janitor at a prominent ministry for thirteen years. Despite his desire to preach, he obeyed the Holy Spirit's conviction to take the cleaning job during a revival event. As he cleaned, he continuously listened to prayer and healing schools led by figures like Gina, Pastor Terri, and Annie. This environment cultivated his spiritual gifts, leading to angelic visitations, a mantle of prayer, and precise prophetic visions, including foresight of the 9/11 terrorist attacks. He learned the intricate workings of a mega-church while remaining steadfast in his behind-the-scenes role.

DIANA'S ACADEMIC SACRIFICES AND THE EBAY MIRACLE: Brandon details the intense struggles his wife, Diana, endured to secure their family's future, particularly after their daughter Megan was diagnosed with severe autism. Diana pursued her higher education online at Oral Roberts University, working late into the night while managing her ministry job and caring for their special-needs child. She remarkably earned both a Bachelor's degree in Business and Accounting and a Master's degree in Taxation with a perfect 4.0 GPA. Following a moral failure by their pastor that resulted in Brandon's layoff, God miraculously provided for them through a prophetic directive to start an eBay business selling thrift store clothing, allowing them to instantly pay a critical bill and sustain their family.

HEARING GOD THROUGH SPORTS AND WEATHER: Brandon explains his unique method of developing his prophetic ear by practicing the presence of the Lord through everyday interests like sports and meteorology. God would accurately reveal the outcomes and exact scores of NBA games, March Madness, the World Series, and the Super Bowl, under the strict condition that Brandon never use this foreknowledge for gambling. Furthermore, Brandon received precise meteorological insights, knowing far in advance the severity of summer heatwaves, the exact number and categories of hurricanes, and specific landfalls for storms like Hurricane Katrina.

END-TIME PROPHECIES AND THE ANTICHRIST SYSTEM: Brandon outlines a detailed prophetic vision concerning the rise of the Antichrist and a global financial shift. He predicts a massive wealth transfer shifting toward the Middle East, specifically involving Saudi Arabia, Iraq, Syria, and Jordan. Brandon warns that the Antichrist will not appear as an obvious villain but rather as an incredibly charismatic and widely liked figure offering compelling solutions to the world's problems. He explicitly ties the incoming Antichrist system to modern technological advancements, predicting a cashless society driven by digital blockchain, cryptocurrency, Starlink, Neuralink, and a payment chip embedded in the hand or forehead.

WEATHER COMMANDS AND SPIRITUAL WARFARE: Brandon shifts into intense spiritual warfare, asserting the believer's authority over atmospheric conditions and demonic forces. He actively commands an abundance of rain over drought-stricken areas like Texas, Kansas, and Florida, while simultaneously binding excessive heat, destructive category hurricanes from the Gulf of Mexico, and long-track twin tornadoes threatening Nebraska and Kansas. He emphasizes that believers must proactively use their faith to dictate weather patterns and prevent natural disasters before they physically manifest.

GLOBAL VISIONS OF AFRICA AND SOUTH AMERICA: Brandon concludes by sharing vivid prophetic visions he received regarding international territories. He visualizes the continent of Africa as a land of stark contrast between lush green pastures and barren wastelands, prophesying a massive revival that will ignite there and sweep across the Middle East and Egypt. In contrast, he perceives significant spiritual opposition in South America, symbolized by a gigantic snapping turtle near the Panama Canal waiting to strike, alongside a vision of a dark, talon-covered fruit resembling a pinecone that represents entrenched witchcraft and demonic strongholds needing to be broken through prayer.
rawData: [May 16, 2025]-[Praying over the volcanos and hurricanes - getting to know us better]-[https://youtu.be/lnU58qYMWIU]-[Hey, good morning family. Praise the Lord. Thank you for joining us today. Well, it's Diana and Brandon again, and we have some things on our heart to talk to you about. I've been trying to make these videos every day, praying with you and seeking the face of the Lord so that we could be this prayer army that God's called us all to be. But there's a lot of people that don't know who we are, where we've come from, and our past, including some of the struggles and successes we've been through. Diana and I have been married for quite a while now, going on twenty-some years. We set out to be in ministry; it seems like my whole life since I was my son's age, I've always wanted to seek the ministry and have some part of it, even if it was behind the scenes. I started traveling and going to Benny Hinn crusades because I saw what he did on TV, and I was like, 'Man, there is something about this. Is this fake? Is this real?' I was just curious as a young kid. So, I saved up my money as I worked, put it aside, and would go to crusades. My dad, or friends and I, would get in the car and drive long distances. I flew to Fort Lauderdale and went to Arkansas and Fort Worth. There were a lot of places we went to just to experience the glory because we were hungry. I experienced the glory of the Lord there. I know he gets a lot of hate now—a lot of people do not like him—and I'm not here to defend him, as I don't know him personally. I just know that I experienced the glory when I was there. So, if I bring up a person's name, don't throw eggs at me and say, 'Well, you're saying his name; he's of the devil,' and all that. I don't believe they're of the devil, or I wouldn't have gone. I only went because I knew in my heart I had a pulling to go and experience the glory, the same way I want to experience it with Billy Burke. I don't chase people; I chase the glory and the anointing. When we got married, I told her, 'Let's go to some crusades.' We traveled around and experienced them on our vacations from our ministry jobs. I just wanted to be where Jesus was. In the back, I was helping because I was a stocky guy—pretty tall and hefty—so I could catch people. A guy named Sam saw me and I told him, 'I'm here to volunteer; I want to usher.' He said, 'Well, you can help the people down off the stage.' I wanted to get as close to the platform as I could so I could see. There were times when they wanted me to catch because there were so many people. When Benny Hinn took his hand like this, the glory of the Lord knocked me back. I couldn't get up off the ground, and I was hooked. I called Diana on the phone and said, 'Hey, you'll never believe what just happened. I just got knocked off my feet. It was amazing.' After the service, I went to the back where a man was eating strawberry cake and strawberries. I'll never forget it. He said, 'Young man, you're the reason I showed up today.' I looked at him like, 'Who is this old guy?' He said, 'The Lord told me I was supposed to meet you. I have a word for you.' He said, 'I'm going to meet twelve of you in the next couple of months. You're end-time people that will bring healing to the nations.' He told me I had a mantle on my life like Kathryn Kuhlman, Oral Roberts, and Benny Hinn. He said, 'I'm supposed to pray for you. My name is Ralph Wilkerson, and I have Melodyland Christian Center.' I didn't know who that was at the time, but I later learned he had a mega-church in Anaheim. He said, 'I'm supposed to lay hands on you and impart a double portion of the anointing on my life onto you by what the Spirit of the Lord has told me to do.' He laid his hands on mine, and suddenly, the fire of the Holy Spirit went through him into me. The palms of my hands felt like I had two hot coals in them. He prayed and prophesied over my future, imparting the glory into me. I was shook; the power of God was all over me. I was forever changed and have never been the same since that impartation. At the time, I was working at a grocery store, sacked groceries for years because I had a lot of learning problems. I did that until a lady named Debbie called and said, 'Brandon, we have a job for you at the ministry if you want to be a janitor here.' I told her, 'I'm not interested. I'm called to preach and prophesy. I am not a janitor.' She said, 'Well, I think you're making a mistake, but if that's your choice, okay.' After I hung up, the Holy Spirit said, 'You take that job and you take it now,' rebuking me for my attitude. I called her back and said, 'Debbie, I feel the Lord has told me to take this job. My flesh doesn't want to, but I'll do it.' It was during a 30-day revival called '30 Days of Glory.' I started cleaning and helping out, and after the meeting, they hired me full-time. Not knowing what God was doing, I started cleaning in the back. They had prayer school every Thursday where they taught people to pray, just like I do with you now. On my breaks, I would sit in the back and listen to a lady named Gina and Pastor Terri. They would pray every Thursday, along with a lady named Annie. I listened to them all the time while I was cleaning. On Mondays they had healing school, and on Thursdays they had prayer. I would listen as they ministered to the sick. This went on for three years. I started taking care of the ministry plans, the ministry, and the balconies. I started going down to the main house of this preacher. I took care of things. I took care of their cars, I cleaned things, I did a lot; I cleaned carpets. So I started getting to go and help out and get things ready for main guest speakers that were coming, like Oral Roberts and a lot of others. That's what I did. As I kept growing there, I started to have more favor, and I would just keep going because the Lord just had His hand on me. Well, I learned to pray. They had a guest speaker come to an all-staff meeting; his name was Reinhard Bonnke. I don't know if very many of you know who Reinhard Bonnke is, but Reinhard Bonnke was there, and he was telling the people thank you because this main ministry supported a lot of his crusades throughout Africa, and he wanted to say thank you for giving him money to help bring in the lost for Jesus. He was praying there, and all of a sudden, he said, 'I feel led to pray for the staff. I feel led to pray for the staff. So staff, if you feel you want to get prayed for, come down to the front, and I'll lay hands on you.' And I think probably everybody went down there. I ran down there because I was like, 'This guy's talking about raising the dead. He's talking about seeing people get healed. He's talking about millions of people getting saved.' And I was like, 'I want a part of this.' So when he came down to the front, he put his hands on top of my head, and he stopped and said, 'I put a double portion of the anointing on my life onto you.' I kid you not, it felt like a bolt of lightning hit me that day and knocked me on my backside again, and in that, I had impartation. I got up, and I was forever—I'm telling you, that was one, besides Ralph Wilkerson, that was a forever-changing experience when Reinhard touched me. I'm telling you all, I still have that fire of the Holy Ghost stirring on the inside of me, and it's something I—you know, as a kid, you wanted to be bad or whatever, you wanted to go out and hang with your friends. But I always had the Holy Ghost always stirring me. I couldn't sin without having that in the back of my mind: 'I've got a call in my life. I have impartation.' It was always something convicting me; I don't know how else to describe it. It was just heavy on me, the Holy Spirit telling me, 'Don't do this. You've got a call. You've got to do this.' And then I started having dreams and visions when I started praying. I was in the back one day at that church and the Lord spoke to me. He said, 'I'm putting a mantle of prayer from this sister that went home.' And if I said her name, everybody would know who she was. But the Lord said, 'I'm going to give you that mantle. And as this comes on you, you will pray and it will be different, and you're going to start to see.' So I started looking up on the internet who this woman was and I started trying to find books and teachings about who she was that the Lord told me about. And I was like, 'Okay, Lord.' So I started studying her. And the more I prayed in the back with Gina and them, it just kept bubbling up on me. And I started seeing things. I started seeing terrorist attacks; I started seeing all kinds of things. I'm talking about when 9/11 happened as a young man. I saw all that; I had it all down to everything that was going on. The Lord showed me and I had specific details of things, and people were like, 'Well, who is this kid?' because I was just a young kid. I just started seeing the spirit that way. And then the more I prayed, the more I sought Him, the more I started seeing angels, the more I started talking, and I had a visitation from Jesus. I've had so many visions and impartations of seeing into the spirit realm that I can't count them all anymore; you lose track. And I don't say that all to brag; I tell you this because I'm trying to tell you who we are. And that's what's happened. When the Lord told me to leave Fort Worth and leave that ministry, we came here. I thought for sure at that time, because of the way I prayed and things were going on with me, that I was going to step right into ministry at a young age. And the Lord called me to be a janitor again. I cleaned toilets at that mega-church, learning how a mega-church functions so I could run it with my eyes closed. I'm just being real. I watched everything and I learned everything I could from the staff to the music, to all of it, how it flowed. I was there every Sunday and every Wednesday. That was my job. I opened it, I closed it, and I set the alarm. I was everything; I walked around and cleaned the bathrooms and got everything done. And that was what I did for 13 years—10 years on staff like that and then the other time I was doing the floors in other campuses. So altogether 13-some years, and it was the longest 13 years of my life. So Diana's going to tell her side, what she's done, and I'll let her talk a little bit. Yeah. So I worked at the ministry too. I did the prisoner letters and counted the offerings and that kind of thing. And then we met at a Bible study—at Jeremy's Bible study. Yeah. And then when we moved here, I worked at the grandson of the main minister's. We met at a Bible study and we've talked ever since. And then when we moved here, when we worked at the other ministry, I counted offerings there as well. And after Megan got diagnosed with autism, she was still in diapers. It was really, really severe. Brendan's told the story; it was a really, really dark time in our life. We're still going through that, but we're seeing progress. And so that's good, ever since she got prayed over by Billy Burke. So, but whenever she was diagnosed at the ministry that we were at, they were telling me that I couldn't work from home and that if I went and got my degree, I might get a promotion, but we would have to pay for it ourselves. So I was really praying about it because I really needed to be home with Megan a lot and it was just a lot. They said you could be at home working part-time and be there for a season because of how bad Megan was, right? But they said one day you're going to have to come back up here. They told me I was going to have to go back up there. So we were really praying about it and felt that the Lord was leading me to go to ORU. They had this program where you could go online, and it was something that I was interested in. So I started doing that, and I would work all day at the ministry and then come home and write papers and essays and all the stuff at ORU, do all my tests, watch everything, and I worked on that until sometimes 2:00 in the morning. And so I did that for four years for my bachelor's. Yeah, four years there at ORU. And don't be modest, she made a 4.0 all the way through college with her bachelor's, by the grace of God. Yes, a 4.0 by the grace of God. Summa Cum Laude, graduated top of the class for business and for accounting. Yeah, got that. Then, because you specialized in business and everything, right? And then after those four years, then she said, 'Brandon, I feel like I'm supposed to keep going.' I said, 'Are you kidding? We just did four years, every night of our life for four years.' I haven't really got to talk to you because I was taking care of Megan and Braden. I told her, "I'm exhausted with this, but if you feel led to go, honey, I'll support you." She said, "Well, it takes three years for my master's, Brandon." Yeah, to do another three years. I thought, "Dear God," because I was thinking it would probably be more beneficial pay-wise. So I got my major and then my master's in accounting and taxation. She made a 4.0 all the way through that. You cannot skip that part, baby. You worked hard. She made straight A's through her master's, got an accounting degree, and was able to specialize in taxation, right? Taxes and doing CPA stuff for ministries. She wanted to do the ministry finances at the church we were working for. That was the goal, to be the head of it because it made good money there. I was just trying to provide for Megan's needs, and that gets really expensive when you're a parent. All her therapists were saying, "You've got to prepare for her future and she needs a savings account." So I was thinking we have to keep going. Maybe that'll help make more money if I get my master's, so that's why I just kept going. There were so many nights that I would literally crawl into bed crying, and I would say I didn't even understand what I was doing in some classes. It was pretty intense. There were classes like that—what I'm talking about. She would have a problem in some of those master's classes that would take up two or three pages, and she'd keep flipping it over and say, "I got it right." I remember you crying. I prayed for you. I cried and cried. There were several classes where I don't know how I did it other than the grace of God. I remember I would just sit there and cry out to God in the shower and say, "Lord, you told me to do this. I need some help here because I don't know what I'm doing in this class." Then I would pray, take a break, go back to the class, and see it in a different light. That's how I made it. Sometimes in the homework I would fail, but I'd make the A. Holy Spirit would help bring things to my remembrance and help my memory during the tests to score higher. I don't know how she did it other than the grace of God because she didn't have a tutor. She watched YouTube videos of someone teaching because the teachers were not very helpful. She watched it online, did the course, and did the assignments, and that's all she had. Sometimes I told her, "I don't know how you're going to do this," but I was going to pray in the Holy Ghost. What really helped was that in high school, I had some really good teachers who taught me how to write essays. I was pretty decent at writing essays going into college. We wrote so many essays for professors, and I got really good at it. The reason I bring up her testimony is that she did all that with two babies. One had special needs so severe that we couldn't sleep, functioning off no sleep and staying up. We did all this for seven years, pushing forward. Now we have a ton of student loans, but we'll get it taken care of. It is a major amount of student loans. Then, when our pastor fell morally, she had just graduated. We were thinking we'd announce she has her degree. I'm so proud of her. I said, "Baby, you're like Rocky! You did it! You knocked down the big Russian guy." I told her, "We can do this now. We're going to make decent money." We were living on food stamps and barely making it the whole time. I told her, "Thank God we're there. Maybe we'll make six figures and be able to pay back these student loans." We were so excited. The next two weeks, she went in and they said, "We have an announcement to make." The pastor we were working for, who told her she could run the finances, fell morally. I got laid off. She kept doing the accounting and the offerings, but I told her we had to get out of there because they weren't going to promote her. They said they couldn't anymore because they laid off so many people; their finances tanked. That happened the year I graduated. God had other plans, which was to do eBay. That all came through her too, because of learning the business side of things. People ask, "How did you learn to hear God the way you hear Him?" Well, I had always seen things since I was a teen. I pray and the Lord shows me things, but I started developing the prophetic ear of really hearing the voice of the Lord. Don't crucify me, but this is just real. Don't get mad when I tell you how I heard. It was always watching sports with the Lord. That may sound crazy, but I would sit on the couch and we would talk about the score of the game. I would pray in the spirit, and the Lord said, "Brandon, I'm going to teach you to hear because you love sports. You love basketball, football, and baseball, and I'm going to talk to you so you can practice my presence through this." He would say, "This is who's going to win and in what quarter." He would tell me each thing step by step. He would tell me in advance who was going to win the World Series and the Super Bowl. I would write it down and tell Diana privately. The Lord said this team would win and who would be in the Super Bowl at the end of the year, including the score. Every single time, it was right. Then he started talking to me about the weather. He would say, "On this day, it's going to rain. This day, a cold front's coming through. In four weeks, this is what's going to happen." He would tell me how much rain we'd get. It was always something, but I think my favorite thing was always the weather. I loved it. He still talks to me about the weather, but not like that. Now we talk about everything because it's like we're on here. But I loved the simplicity of the relationship back then, how I practiced His presence and we would talk about sports. My main thing was the NBA Thunder. I would always tell him, and so much so, March Madness came and I would pick the teams that were going to win for March Madness college basketball. But the Lord told me, 'If you ever bet or gamble off of what I'm telling you, I'll never speak to you this way again. This is something private between me and you. You can learn this because you can learn and practice my presence and hear my voice.' Brandon, this was something that you did not talk about at the time. I never told anybody; I was always private about it, and it was something that Diana and I knew, and my son. It was always fun to us. We always knew when the weather was coming because God always talked about it. I would hear in April how the weather was going to be all through the summer. It was really cool, I'm telling you. I would write things down. I literally told her, 'By this time, this is how the summer will be,' and I'd tell her the temperatures. The Lord would tell me how many hurricanes would be in the summer, how many major hurricanes would hit America, and how many major tornadoes would come. He would always tell me far in advance. I would know exactly how many hurricanes and the category and where they would hit precisely at the location when they would forecast a week in advance. I'd see it develop in the Gulf of Mexico or out in the Caribbean, and He would say, 'This is where that hurricane's going to hit, and it's going to hit this town.' He would tell me, and I would tell people—I would try to hint around to them. I would say, 'Hey, this is where I believe it's going to hit. They're all saying it's going to hit here, but...' And then whenever Katrina happened, I knew. There are just things that the Lord talks to you about, and I'm just being real. This is how I learned. So Diana and I just kept practicing His presence. The more that happened, the more I got confident in hearing Him, and the more He started talking to me and developing other things. Then He wanted us to eBay. To make a long story short, we started doing that and He started telling me where to go to get stuff out of thrift stores. We started doing eBay because we had a bill that was due and we didn't have the money to pay it because he had just gotten laid off. We were trying to come up with a way to not have to borrow the money. I was praying about it, and I felt in my spirit the Holy Spirit say, 'Just go ahead and start reselling clothes from thrift stores.' We only had $100. So I told Brandon, 'I think the Lord's telling me that we should take that $100 and I'll go into the thrift store and look for the clothes that He leads me to, that I think I can make a profit on. Then we can come up with the money and I'll put everything on auction.' That's what we did, and it exploded. The Lord blessed our socks off. We were able to pay that bill within a day. I look at where God has brought us from. I'm telling you all, God is no respecter of persons. What He did for us in our little journey, whether it's boring to you all or not, I think it's good to talk about it because a lot of people have no clue. We're not just people who come on YouTube; we've had a story. Everybody has a story and we fought it out. We've stuck it out. We've been together through very trying times. We're still married, we still love each other, and we're passionate about trying to help you all. Our heart is to preach to the nations, to lay hands on the sick and do the Great Commission, to do everything that God's called us to do even with all the words that have been given to us. When she graduated at ORU—she graduated, not we, though I feel like I'm part of it—the dean of ORU gave her a prophetic word and laid hands on her. He said, 'The Lord told me to tell you: you're a prophet to the prophets. You and your husband will walk in a very strong prophetic anointing in the days ahead.' That was the only word we had ever gotten that told us we are prophetic people. He said, 'You'll be a prophet to the prophets.' I thought, 'What is that?' He laid hands on her and prayed for her. I wasn't able to go to the graduation because Megan was so bad; I had to sit out in the car while she went to graduate. That was at the honors banquet. I went to the honors banquet first and he couldn't even come in because he had to be in the car with the kids because Megan was... it was too hard. She would have thrown a huge fit. I sat out in the parking lot and prayed in the spirit for her while she went in there and had dinner by herself. I was really disappointed that night, but it's just part of the pressure. Brandon was like, 'You've got to go. You earned this.' They gave her medals and all this cool stuff. I told her, 'You can't miss out on this. You worked too hard.' So as bad as it was that she had to go by herself, she went. She didn't know anybody, but she went. We've been through some stuff, y'all, but we're still standing. No matter how the waves of life try to knock you down, it's the fight on the inside. It's like that little airbag clown that you keep punching and he keeps standing. You say, 'Why does he keep getting back up?' Because he's standing on the inside. That clown is standing on the inside. He keeps getting back up. You keep punching the airbag because he's standing on the inside. There's something about that. I believe it's people who stand on the inside. No matter how bad life tries to knock you on your hiney and these waves keep coming after you, Diana and I keep fighting through it. This is just barely scratching the surface of our testimony. There's been a lot of pain in the process. People say, 'Well, you need counseling.' Jesus has been my counselor. I don't need a shrink. I've got the Word. I may have battle scars. You may look at me and go, 'Brandon, you look like you've been through the wringer.' Yes, I have. I have been through hell, but I've kept walking. I refuse to camp there. I refuse to be a victim of things that have happened. As I told some family members, 'If you lived my life back then for a week, you'd be ready to jump off a cliff.' Not many people could have lived it. It was by the grace of God. And I know there's a lot of you out there that have a worse testimony than us, that have been through trying times. We see your letters. I cry over them. I see some of these letters and what you're going through and I go, "Oh God, I feel it. It's real." I just wanted to give you all some hope that if God can do what he has done through dying and walk us through the valley of the shadow of death a lot of times, feeling like his rod and his staff comfort me—that's the kind of God I believe we serve: he is able to bring us out of the pit. And it may be that we're still walking out of it. We're still coming through the storm, but we're standing up on the inside. By the way, my youngest made me this shirt. I told her, "Daddy, will you wear that shirt after I made it for you?" And I said, "Yes, not only will I wear it, but I'll wear it on my video and everybody on YouTube can see my shirt, baby." So I wore it and she was so excited. She grinned from ear to ear when she said, "Daddy, you're really going to wear it on the internet." So, she made it for me. She sent me a tie-dye. Diana has one to match. I should have worn mine. She wore hers yesterday, so it's dirty. So, we're matching. We have matching tie-dyes. My little Paisley—the one that raised the dog from the dead, that was out praying over that dog that I stepped on and killed—if you ever hear that testimony, that little firecracker, she's the one that made this. She's my little faith buddy. She's a praying machine, isn't she? She's been baptized in the Holy Ghost since she was three years old. Whenever I was praying at night, Braden sits in with me and Diana was sitting there. Megan and dying a lot of times would go put Megan to bed because Megan wouldn't go to sleep without Diana laying by her. But I put Paisley on my lap or Braden and I sit there and just pray, and the fire of God comes on the kids and then they get baptized in the Holy Ghost and they start praying with me. It's the way it's been. Braden's been baptized in the Holy Ghost since I think he was two. It's just the way it is. When you pray and you're a praying family, we just believe in the baptism of the Holy Ghost. And we just believe that Jesus Christ is the same yesterday, today, and forever. It's real to us, and it's exciting to walk things out by faith. So, let's take communion today. I hope that video blessed you all. It was kind of different, but we thought we'd talk about some of our past. And you know what? Our past is our past, and we're thankful for what God's brought us: a brand new future tomorrow. We're a work in progress, folks, as you can see. And so, we're going to take communion right now. I don't want to keep drawing this out. We didn't want to keep it and make it boring for y'all, but we wanted to tell people where we come from. So, let's take communion and see if the Lord shows us anything. There's so much going on right now with the earthquakes, with all the stuff happening in the Middle East this week. Wow. We've had an amazing week, have we not? With all this stuff, I go, "Wow." I told Braden, "Every day, this is getting really intense." I told you the labor pains would start to become more and more. We would see more and more in the news with the signs of the times—basically what I told you all before even President Trump headed over to the Middle East—that we would see a rising of the finances of the Middle East.
